news.title: France vs Spain Live Today Could End One Nation’s World Cup Dream
news.lede: France vs Spain live today will decide who reaches the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final. The semi-final takes place on Tuesday, July 14, 2026, at Dallas Stadium in Arlington, Texas. Kickoff is scheduled for 8:00 PM BST, 3:00 PM EDT, and 2:00 PM local time. France enters the match with the tournament’s best attack, having scored 16 goals in six matches.

news.title: World Cup predictions: Expert picks for Argentina-England, Spain-France
news.lede: The 2026 World Cup semifinals feature two matches soccer fans dreamed about before the tournament began. Spain and and Argentina vs. England pits four powerhouses against one another, with the game's biggest stars on the field together to decide the winner of soccer's most important trophy. The final step before the World Cup ends, begins on Tuesday, July 14 when France and Spain meet in Arlington, Texas, with a spotlight on France's Kylian Mbappe and Spain's Lamine Yamal as they renew their La Liga rivalry on a grand stage.

news.title: One thing you're looking forward to when club football returns
news.lede: The World Cup is doing a fantastic job of filling the Premier League void, but there is nothing quite like club football. As the tournament in North America heads towards its climax, there is a Sunderland fanbase waiting for the 2026-27 season with anticipation. So, it has got us thinking: what are you looking forward to most about club football resuming?

news.title: What Is W26 Crypto? The World Cup 2026 Memecoin Bringing Football Fans On-Chain
news.lede: W26 is a community-driven Solana memecoin built around the 2026 FIFA World Cup, allowing football fans to engage in on-chain memes, tournament speculation, social interaction, and trading. It is not an official product of FIFA, so it promises no voting rights, merchandise, or team ownership. This W26 review discusses the coin’s mechanics, tokenomics, price drivers, risks, and ways to acquire it. A unique blend of sports fandom and speculative trading makes W26 appealing to crypto enthusiasts who would not have otherwise engaged with either.

news.title: Close encounters: England have to crack Argentina’s ability to prevent close-range shots
news.lede: Argentina’s underlying defensive process has been stronger than the outcomes so England must make potent use of their set pieces. Argentina’s defence has not always looked befitting of World Cup champions this summer. Cape Verde, Egypt and Jordan have collectively scored five goals against them, with their only clean sheets coming against Algeria and Austria. The ease with which relative minnows have broken the backline of Lionel Scaloni’s side should offer ample encouragement to Jude Bellingham and Harry Kane.

news.title: War, antics and vitriol – the England v Argentina rivalry is real but now it is only about football
news.lede: Mexico 1986, France 1998, Japan 2002 … this is a match that transcends, with Argentina head coach Lionel Scaloni calling for the focus to be on football, not the Falklands. After confirmation that match 102, one of the World Cup semi-finals, would be England v Argentina, the 1982 Falklands/Malvinas conflict was mentioned at Lionel Scaloni’s press conference. “No, no, no,” the Argentina head coach tut-tutted emphatically. “This is just a football match.

news.lede: Our cartoonist sets the scene before the semi-finals, with Thomas Tuchel’s team taking on old rivals Argentina. Buy David’s cartoons | Some of his favourite works. And his latest book, Chaos in the Box: get it now.

news.title: Lionel Messi’s first meeting with England will be a contest of will and aura
news.lede: The Messi arc reaches a decisive point in Atlanta – passage to a third final or an exit to test the limits of Argentina’s love for their unassuming athlete-genius. Wednesday night, Atlanta Stadium, 101 games down, three left to play, and finally it makes sense. Bring on The Countdown, that moment just before kick-off in every one of those quietly fascinating World Cup matches where suddenly the world’s most excited man is bellowing over the PA system in a state of outraged, crowing transport, like the last voice you’ll ever hear before the American century explodes in a ball of inanity, fried chicken and porn. …” the world’s most excited man shouts, prelude to some cautious rolling possession, maybe an early back-pass, and an agreeable reminder that the game itself will not be stage managed.
